title: Vault annotation (venture hub frontmatter)
description: The HAND layer of the portfolio catalog. Lives in Obsidian: vault/Ideaverse/Cortxt/Verticals/<venture>/<venture>.md. Carries only what cannot be derived from reality — judgement. Reality (existence, repo, deploy, commits, issues) is derived by scripts/derive_catalog.py and merged on top; NEVER hand-written here. Enum values that appear both here and in enums.json are duplicated for standalone validity — enums.json remains the single source of truth and vault_reader.py cross-checks them at load time.

node.description: JOIN KEY to the CNS catalog. A catalog slug, or the literal 'none' for a pre-gate idea that has no node yet. Referential integrity (does this slug exist?) is NOT enforceable by JSON Schema — vault_reader.py checks it.

type.description: venture = in the portfolio funnel. idea = pre-gate, lives in Verticals/_pipeline/, node is 'none'.

owner.description: Who is accountable. Backstage makes owner required for the same reason: automation can never establish it.

steps_done.description: The MANUAL steps you have ticked (recipe steps with `check: manual` — 'talked to 5 customers', 'core flow works'). The machine measures everything it can; these are the ones that need eyes. The phase itself is NEVER written here — it is derived from these plus reality (lab/scripts/phase_derive.py).

phase_override.description: Escape hatch: force a phase when the derivation is wrong. Reconcile LOGS the disagreement rather than hiding it — a persistent override means the deriver needs fixing, not that you need a bigger hammer.

gate_decision.description: AXIS 2 — whether we keep investing. Stage-Gate (Cooper). NEVER derivable: an act of will, not a measurement. This is the field the recipe lacked — which is why nothing ever got killed. A venture with gate_decision: kill is dead regardless of how far the build came.

gate_date.description: When that gate decision was taken. Its age is what makes a stale 'go' visible.

next_gate.description: What this venture must SHOW to earn its next go. If you cannot write this, you are not running a gate — you are hoping.

north_star.description: The one metric that says this is working.

kill_criteria.description: What would make you stop. The literature's warning: gates degenerate into rubber stamps where Kill is never chosen. Written thresholds are the antidote — and reconcile shows how old they are.

next_action.description: The single next move. Reconcile compares its age against repo activity — a three-week-old next_action beside 40 new commits is probably a lie.

last_reviewed.description: The staleness clock. This is the ONLY defence against the hand layer rotting: reconcile knows when reality moved, this says when you last looked. Prior art solves the 'file vanished' case, never the 'file lies' case.

url_live.description: DERIVED from Vercel/Railway — do not hand-write. Present here only so a note can override a bad derivation.

url_repo.description: DERIVED from GitHub — do not hand-write. Same override caveat as url_live.

screenshots.description: Product artifacts under Artifacts/media/ — a deck without a product shot is a pitch without a product.

allOf.description: A pre-gate idea has no catalog node; a venture must have one.
A venture is real code — it must be backed by a catalog node. (The phase is derived, so this cannot be keyed on it; being a `venture` at all is the claim that matters.)
A kill must record when it was decided — otherwise it is not a decision, just neglect. And an undated kill can never go stale, which is exactly how a dead venture keeps quietly costing you.
